skill.name: agent-browser
skill.description: Browser automation for AI agents via inference.sh. Navigate web pages, interact with elements using @e refs, take screenshots, record video. Capabilities: web scraping, form filling, clicking, typing, drag-drop, file upload, JavaScript execution. Use for: web automation, data extraction, testing, agent browsing, research. Triggers: browser, web automation, scrape, navigate, click, fill form, screenshot, browse web, playwright, headless browser, web agent, surf internet, record video
skill.summary: AI-powered browser automation using Playwright. Navigate, click, fill forms, and extract data from web pages with compact element references.
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 607 potential issues, but all are false positives after manual evaluation. The skill is browser automation documentation for the inference.sh platform. External command patterns exist only in markdown code example blocks showing belt CLI usage. Network URLs are example/placeholder domains for documentation. Filesystem paths are markdown relative links and standard bash patterns. The allowed-tools constraint (Bash belt *) limits execution to belt commands only. No malicious intent or hidden functionality was found.

low_findings.title: External commands in documentation code blocks
low_findings.description: Shell command patterns detected in markdown code examples. These are belt CLI invocations in documentation, not freely executable scripts. The allowed-tools constraint limits execution to belt commands only.

low_findings.title: Example URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs found in documentation examples. All URLs use placeholder domains (example.com) or reference the inference.sh platform documentation. No unauthorized network endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: Filesystem path patterns in documentation
low_findings.description: Path traversal sequences detected are relative markdown links between documentation files (e.g., ../SKILL.md). Standard device file access (/dev/null) is used for error suppression in bash examples.

content.user_title: Automate web browsers with AI agent control
content.value_statement: Browser automation tools require complex CSS selectors and DOM parsing. This skill lets AI agents navigate and interact with web pages using simple @e element references through the inference.sh platform.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to web pages and capture interactive element references with @e refs
Fill forms, click buttons, select dropdowns, and upload files using element refs
Take viewport and full-page screenshots of any web page
Record browser sessions as video for debugging and documentation
Execute JavaScript on pages to extract data or manipulate the DOM
Route traffic through HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 proxies for geo-testing
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (belt) to be installed and authenticated
Cannot interact with elements inside iframes directly
Video recording consumes memory and only available after session closes
Snapshots limited to 50 interactive elements per page

use_cases.title: Form submission automation
use_cases.description: Automate complex multi-step form submissions for testing or data entry workflows.

use_cases.title: Web data extraction
use_cases.description: Scrape structured data from web pages using JavaScript execution and snapshot output.

use_cases.title: Authenticated session testing
use_cases.description: Log into web applications once and reuse authenticated sessions across multiple automation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic page navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Open a web page, identify interactive elements, and describe what actions are possible.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use to understand a web page structure

prompt_templates.title: Form fill and submit
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to a login page, fill in the email and password fields, submit the form, and confirm successful login.

prompt_templates.title: Data extraction with screenshots
prompt_templates.prompt: Open a search results page, extract all result titles and links, and take a full-page screenshot.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-page workflow with video recording
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a new browser session with video recording enabled. Navigate through a 3-step signup flow, take screenshots at each step, then close the session and retrieve the video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Recording a user onboarding flow for documentation

output_examples.input: Open example.com and describe what you see
output_examples.output: The page loaded successfully. I can see the main heading, a navigation link, and the page footer. Interactive elements are available via @e refs that I can use for further actions.

output_examples.input: Log in to the admin dashboard and take a screenshot
output_examples.output: I opened the login page, filled in the credentials, and submitted the form. Login was successful - the dashboard loaded. I captured a full-page screenshot showing the navigation menu and summary statistics.

output_examples.input: Extract all product names and prices from the catalog page
output_examples.output: I navigated to the catalog page and used a snapshot to identify product elements. I found 12 products with their names and prices. I can present them in a table format or save the data for further processing.

best_practices: Always re-snapshot after navigation steps to get valid @e element references
Close browser sessions when done to free server resources and retrieve recorded video
Store session IDs in variables instead of re-parsing them for each command
anti_patterns: Using stale element refs after page navigation - always re-snapshot
Hardcoding credentials in automation scripts instead of using environment variables
Recording video for sensitive sessions that contain passwords or personal data

faq.question: What is the @e ref system?
faq.answer: When you open a page, the skill returns compact element references like @e1, @e2. These let you click, fill, or interact with elements without writing CSS selectors.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh CLI tool (belt). Install it with: npx skills add belt-sh/cli

faq.question: Can I log in to websites with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports login flows, OAuth, and 2FA. Sessions maintain cookies so you stay logged in across steps.

faq.question: How do I record a video of browser interactions?
faq.answer: Pass record_video: true when opening a session. The video is returned when you close the session. This is useful for debugging and documentation.

faq.question: Can I use a proxy for browsing?
faq.answer: Yes. Pass proxy_url and optional proxy_username and proxy_password when opening a session. SOCKS5 and HTTP proxies are supported.

faq.question: What happens if an element ref expires?
faq.answer: Refs become invalid after page navigation. Run the snapshot function to get fresh refs. The interact response includes a fresh snapshot so you can chain actions.
